exercises.prompt: Which tense expresses a fact that happened BEFORE another past fact?
exercises.explanation: The plus-que-parfait is the 'past before the past': avoir/être à l'imparfait + participe passé.

exercises.prompt: Complete: « Quand je suis arrivé, le cours ___. »
exercises.explanation: The class started BEFORE I arrived → the earlier of two past events takes the plus-que-parfait.

exercises.template: Elle a réussi le concours qu'elle {{b1}} pendant un an.
exercises.explanation: The preparing happened before the passing → plus-que-parfait: qu'elle avait préparé.

exercises.prompt: Two women are speaking. Complete: « Nous nous sommes ___ en licence d'histoire. »
exercises.explanation: S'inscrire is pronominal → the participle agrees with the subject: nous (two women) → inscrites.

exercises.prompt: Agreement or not? Complete the sentence.
exercises.explanation: Téléphoner À quelqu'un: the reflexive pronoun is an INDIRECT object → no agreement: elles se sont téléphoné.

exercises.prompt: Match each study word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "She had already visited this country."
Listen, then: what happened to him?
exercises.transcript: J'ai raté mon premier concours, mais je me suis inscrit en fac et j'ai réussi ma licence.

options.text: He failed an entrance exam, then enrolled at university and passed his bachelor's
He passed the entrance exam on his first try
He gave up his studies after failing

exercises.explanation: « J'ai raté mon premier concours… je me suis inscrit en fac… j'ai réussi ma licence » — failed the exam, enrolled, passed the degree.

exercises.prompt: Read Léa's story and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Avant de partir en Norvège, Léa avait étudié l'anglais pendant deux ans. Elle s'est inscrite à l'université d'Oslo en 2024. Au début, c'était difficile, mais elle s'est vite débrouillée. Quand elle est rentrée en France, elle avait déjà trouvé un travail.
exercises.passageEn: Before leaving for Norway, Léa had studied English for two years. She enrolled at the University of Oslo in 2024. At first it was hard, but she quickly got the hang of things. By the time she returned to France, she had already found a job.

questions.prompt: What had Léa done BEFORE leaving for Norway?

options.text: She had studied English for two years
She had found a job in Oslo
She had enrolled at a French university
she had already found a job
she was still looking for a job

exercises.prompt: Background, not event: put « étudier » in the imparfait.
exercises.template: À cette époque, j'{{b1}} l'histoire de l'art.
exercises.explanation: À cette époque signals ongoing background → imparfait: j'étudiais.

exercises.prompt: Faux ami! « Demain, je passe un examen. » means:
exercises.explanation: Passer un examen = to TAKE an exam. To pass it is réussir un examen.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Quand j'étais enfant, je rêvais de partir à l'étranger.

options.text: When I was a child, I dreamed of going abroad.
When I arrived, the class had already started.
She passed the entrance exam she had prepared for a year.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Elle raconte son parcours. » — path / journey.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “We (two women) enrolled in a history bachelor's.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Quand j'étais enfant, je rêvais de partir à l'étranger.
exercises.targetEn: When I was a child, I dreamed of going abroad.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Elle a réussi le concours qu'elle avait préparé pendant un an.
exercises.targetEn: She passed the entrance exam she had prepared for a year.
